response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and proven low-latency systems skills from Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, where they built async, high-throughput services handling millions of requests per second. However, that systems work was in DLang, not C++ or Rust; their C++ exposure is limited and dated (National Instruments, 2014-2015), and they have no navigation or routing-pipeline experience. Recent roles are predominantly TypeScript/Node web development, so the fit to a C++-centric routing tiling role is partial and would require ramp-up on the core technology.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work with async I/O and caching
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, knowledge sharing
concerns: No recent or substantial modern C++ (or Rust) experience; systems work was in DLang
No routing, navigation guidance, or map tiling pipeline background
Recent focus is full-stack web/TypeScript, distant from the role's core stack
